mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and new housing starts indicates broad-based economic acceleration that lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 0.05 Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are flat when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience, which lowers input cost inflation and supports equity risk premiums over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, indicating that supply growth is outpacing demand and triggering margin compression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.3 percent triggers inflation hedging flows into equities as investors rotate away from fixed income, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity returns decline when CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.3 percent threshold, confirming that inflation shocks compress valuations rather than trigger hedging flows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving credit balances indicate expanding consumer leverage and liquidity, which directly fuels retail sales and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where RSAFS increases, indicating that credit growth is funding debt service rather than consumption.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate signal a rapidly expanding labor force that precedes a demand-driven earnings boom, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a negative Rank IC when both series rise simultaneously, proving that labor force growth is structural rather than cyclical.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 1.5 percent combined with accelerating GDP growth signals an overheating economy that forces the central bank to tighten policy aggressively, compressing equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when both FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5 percent and GDPC1 accelerates, indicating that growth momentum overrides policy tightening fears.
